"Above all things have fervent charity among yourselves," Peter said to the saints, "for charity shall cover the multitude of sins." (1 Pet. 4:8.)

The Lord has counseled to "clothe yourselves with the bond of charity, as with a mantle, which is the bond of perfectness and peace" (D&C 88:125). A mantle is a robe or a cloak and the term is often used symbolically to express a covering that characterizes a trait or the authority which an individual possesses.
